meta_001: Tarot Mind Cards — 3 cards that mirror your mind today
meta_002: You don’t draw fortunes here — you draw questions. 22 ancient symbols, one honest mirror. Pick your 3.
html_006: 22 ancient symbols, turned into a mirror for your mind.
html_008: ① Pick the theme that’s been sitting on your mind
html_009: ② Out of 22 cards, choose the
html_012: ③ They open in order: Where I Am → What I Face → The Way Forward.
html_018: ※ Tarot won’t predict your future — and it’s not supposed to. This app reworks the old symbols into
html_020: . The answers come from you, not the cards.
html_031: Don’t overthink it — the first card that catches your eye is yours ✨
html_033: 🔄 Draw again with a new theme
html_034: 🧪 Dopamine Lab — more tests this way →
js_003: The power to step before the math works out. ‘Not ready’ is often just another name for ‘brave enough to start’.
js_004: If failing were totally fine, what would you start today?
js_007: Every tool you need is already on your desk. What’s missing usually isn’t skill — it’s deciding to use it.
js_008: What’s one ability you have but never use?
js_011: Your head says it’s complicated. Your gut already knows. Turn down the noise and you’ll hear it.
js_012: What’s the answer you secretly already know?
js_015: You can’t pour from an empty cup. Taking care of yourself isn’t a luxury — it’s the capital behind every relationship you have.
js_016: What have you been filling yourself with lately?
js_019: Rules can be the fence that protects you — or the wall that cages you. Time to inspect the boundary.
js_020: Name one rule that protects you, and one that cages you.
js_023: Nobody wins alone anymore. One map from someone who walked the road first can save you months.
js_024: Who should you be asking for advice right now?
js_027: Choosing feels hard not because there’s no answer — but because you haven’t decided what you’re willing to give up.
js_028: Honestly — which way is your heart already leaning?
js_031: The direction is set. Now it’s about speed. Gather your scattered energy into one lane.
js_032: What’s the one thing you should hit the gas on right now?
js_035: Real strength isn’t suppressing the lion inside — it’s taming it. Stop fighting yourself. Start negotiating.
js_036: What does the lion inside you (that urge, that anger) actually want?
js_039: A lantern only shows up in the dark. Sometimes going offline is what points you in the right direction.
js_040: When was the last time you were completely, truly alone?
js_043: You can’t control everything — the wisdom is knowing which wheels you can turn, and which ones turn on their own.
js_044: One thing to hold on to, one thing to let flow — what are they?
js_047: The scales aren’t just for judging other people. Have you been handing yourself the harshest verdicts?
js_048: Where are you generous with everyone — except yourself?
js_051: Pausing isn’t falling behind. Some views only appear when you’re hanging upside down.
js_052: Flip your situation upside down — what suddenly looks like an opportunity?
js_055: Something has to end for the next thing to begin. This card isn’t about loss — it’s about clearing the seat.
js_056: What do you keep holding, even though you know it’s over?
js_059: Life is a mixing art — like blending two cups of water. Pour off the excess, top up what’s running low.
js_060: In your life right now: one thing that’s too much, one that’s not enough?
js_063: Look closer at that chain — it isn’t actually locked. Maybe you’re not unable to quit. Maybe you don’t want to let go.
js_064: That thing you can’t quit — what’s the real reward it gives you?
js_067: When what you built comes down, you finally see the foundation. Sometimes collapse is the fastest renovation.
js_068: From the rubble — what’s the one thing worth picking up?
js_071: You can’t touch a star, but it still gives you direction. Right now you don’t need a big win — just a small thing to believe in.
js_072: What’s one thing you’d like to believe in again?
js_075: Anxiety isn’t a malfunction — it’s a signal. Shadows look huge in moonlight, but the real thing is smaller than you think.
js_076: What is that anxiety trying to tell you?
js_079: This is you shining without trying. Don’t let today’s small good things walk past unnoticed.
js_080: What made you smile today, even for a second?
js_083: A chance to re-score the past has arrived. You can’t change what happened — but you can change what it means.
js_084: If you got one more shot, what would you do differently?
js_087: One full cycle, complete. Before the next journey, give what’s finished a proper goodbye.
js_088: What farewell would you like to give the thing you finished well?

js_093: What you’ll need to face to move on

js_102: The cards didn’t give you answers — they left you 3 questions instead. Answer just one of them yourself today. That’s the real reading.
